howItWorks.intro: This pasta water ratio calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Water Ratio
rules.explanation: Approximately 1 quart of water per 4 oz of dried pasta.

rules.name: Cooked Weight
rules.formula: Cooked Weight = Dry Weight x 2 (dried pasta)
rules.explanation: Dried pasta roughly doubles in weight when cooked. Fresh pasta gains about 50%.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.q: How much water do you need per pound of pasta?
faq.a: The traditional Italian rule is 4 quarts (1 gallon) of water per pound of dried pasta. This gives pasta room to move and cook evenly. For smaller amounts, use at least 2 quarts minimum to maintain water temperature when pasta is added.

faq.q: How much salt should I add to pasta water?
faq.a: Use about 1-2 tablespoons of kosher salt per quart of water. The water should taste pleasantly salty, like the sea. This is the only chance to season the pasta itself — sauce alone cannot make up for unsalted pasta.
